channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Social Security Administration bans reading 'general news' at work
item.description: The Social Security Administration (SSA) has banned reading “general news” websites at work, The Hill has learned.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City, rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AWS Forms Agentic AI Unit to Promote Autonomous Technology on Its Platform
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) customers can expect more agentic AI solutions with the formation of a dedicated unit to promote autonomous technology on its platform, led by Swami Sivasubramanian.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Audiomack became an unlikely Spotify competitor
item.description: Kendrick Lamar. Drake. Lady Gaga. The charts of music streaming services pretty much all look the same these days, with familiar names dominating the top spots—except on up-and-coming Spotify competitor Audiomack. The current No.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Without being there, Apple hits Mobile World Congress
item.description: If you overlook the introduction of the iPhone 16e with its own Apple-designed 5G modem along with the release of new iPads, Macs and processors, you might argue that Apple didn’t make an impression at this year’s Mobile World Congress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple has quietly updated its guidance on how to clean your AirPods, and suggests you buy a kit… from Belkin
item.description: You have an AirPods cleaning kit, right? Oh you don't? That's OK, Apple's got new some advice on that.
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: Lady Gaga’s Spotify press conference is being live streamed today – here’s where you can watch Spotify’s big step forward in fan inclusion
item.description: In a first-of-its-kind event, Spotify and Lady Gaga are marking a step towards fan inclusion with streaming services. Here's where you can stream Gaga's press conference ahead of her new album release.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Google Cloud unveils new AI Protection security tools, no matter which model you use
item.description: Google Cloud's new AI Protection suite offers features designed to mitigate risks across AI workloads and data.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to catch a Gravid Bowfin in Monster Hunter Wilds
item.description: Here's where to find a Gravid Bowfin in Monster Hunter Wilds, including some info on catching it once you do.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Majority of voters don't want government to make decisions using AI: Poll
item.description: A majority of U.S. voters do not want the government using artificial intelligence (AI) to make decisions, according to a new poll from Americans for Responsible Innovation (ARI) shared first with The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Shazam now makes it super-easy to add identified songs to a Spotify or Apple Music playlist – here’s how it works
item.description: Apple's new Shazam update makes it easier for you to sync your Shazam-searched songs to Spotify and Apple Music - here's how.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: First Fujifilm GFX100RF images leaked in build-up to expected reveal – here’s what they tell us about the unique premium compact camera
item.description: Leaked images seemingly reveal the top and rear of the rumored Fujifilm GFX100RF, the anticipated 100MP medium-format compact.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is your EA just IT? Unlocking the value of enterprise architecture
item.description: Enterprise architecture (EA) has evolved beyond governance and documentation. Today, it’s a business accelerator driving efficiency, accelerating digital transformation, and shaping competitive advantage. When executed strategically, EA can: Reduce costs through optimized IT investments.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The key new features in .NET 10
item.description: The first preview of the next long-term support release of .NET is now available. Like previous even-numbered releases, .NET 10 builds on features introduced in the current short-term release, with a focus on performance and reliability improvements. As .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Utah just passed the country’s first age-verification bill for app stores. But the fight isn’t over
item.description: On Wednesday, Utah became the first state in the country to pass legislation mandating that app stores verify users’ ages and get parental consent for certain activity on minors’ accounts.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Apple appeals UK encryption backdoor demand
item.description: Apple has publicly been pushing back against a UK order that demands it create encryption backdoors, and it has now formally appealed that order to the UK Investigatory Powers Tribunal.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JetZero teams with Delta to take the first blended-wing body commercial jetliner closer to reality
item.description: It’s tough enough bringing the first major redesign of a commercial jetliner to fruition—especially when it looks more like something out of The X-Files than a travel brochure.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Why these former rival founders are teaming up to revive Digg
item.description: Mid-aughts news aggregator Digg is making a comeback, thanks to a pairing that would have seemd unlikely when the site debuted in 2004: Digg founder Kevin Rose and a former corporate rival, Reddit cofounder and former CEO Alexis Ohanian.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: C++ founder champions profiles for memory safety
item.description: C++ memory safety issues have come under heat recently, with rising interest in projects like TrapC and Safe C++ that attempt to tackle the issue. But C++ designer Bjarne Stroustrup remains steadfast in promoting profiles as essential for safety in C++.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal judge blocks drastic funding cuts to medical research
item.description: A federal judge is preventing the Trump administration from drastically cutting medical research funding.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump calls CHIPS Act ‘horrible,’ wants to defund it
item.description: In his address to Congress last night, President Donald J. Trump called the CHIPS Act “horrible,” and said whatever money hasn’t been spent should now be used to reduce the national debt. The CHIPS Act, enacted into law in 2022 under then-President Joseph R. Biden Jr.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Judge denies Musk's move against OpenAI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SolarWinds buys Squadcast to speed incident response
item.description: Observability provider SolarWinds announced this week it had signed an agreement to acquire San Francisco-based Squadcast and its incident response technology for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Employee screening data breach exposes 3.3 million records
item.description: Major employee screening company data breach affects 3.3 million people. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains what happened and gives five ways to protect your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Electronic employee monitoring reaches an all-time high
item.description: Monitoring of both remote and in-office employees is at an all-time high, a trend highlighted by federal workers being told to report their weekly accomplishments. A study by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) found that 80% of companies are monitoring remote or hybrid workers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Improving intelligent document processing with generative AI
item.description: One of the earliest digital transformation drivers was digitalization, in which organizations converted paper-based processes to digitized workflows. In ideal circumstances, paper documents became web or mobile forms, and workflows replaced handoffs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Teradata Adds Vector Support to Enhance Generative AI Capabilities
item.description: Teradata's new Enterprise Vector Store enables retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use cases, such as agentic AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The 2025 Terminator? Lab-grown muscle brings biohybrid robot hand to life
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how lab-grown muscles power a biohybrid hand for scalable prosthetics and research.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How eBPF is changing container networking
item.description: “No man is an island,” wrote John Donne. But containers certainly are. They are created as islands in and of themselves, packaged with all they need to operate independently—code, dependencies, and runtime. This autonomy is great for security but not so great for networking.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Who needs Google technology? Probably not you
item.description: Clearly Google is doing something right. Although Google Cloud’s revenue still lags AWS and Microsoft Azure, it’s growing much faster (albeit on a smaller base). But that’s not the real story of its growth. The story is that it’s growing at all.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: President Trump announced Sunday that the U.S. would be creating a crypto reserve, to boost the growing industry.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Enabling this phone setting reduces anxiety, Gen Z says
item.description: It can create "a buffer against unwanted communications and potential social pressures," a psychotherapist said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Extra cash with no catch? Spotting retiree job scams
item.description: Reports of task scams have significantly risen in the past four years, according to the Federal Trade Commission. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some red flags.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Investment research data breach exposes 12 million customers
item.description: A recent ransomeware attack targeted Zacks, an American investment firm, and a cybercriminal claimed to have stolen records for millions of customers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5
